events.name: Sand Burial
events.gmPrompt: The evening wind picks up without warning. Within minutes it is a blinding, howling gale. Sand stings exposed skin and piles against anything that stays still. Everything not tied down is moving.
events.checkContext: Survival check to secure the camp before the storm buries it.
events.gmGuidance: This is wind and sand, not a magical storm. The solution is physical: lash gear down, cover packs with tarps, weight tent stakes. escalate without a watch because nobody notices the wind building until it is too late. The storm lasts 2-3 hours.
events.description: The evening wind picks up without warning, quickly howling into a blinding gale. A violent sandstorm threatens to bury everything in camp.

onTriumph.narrative: You read the wind shift early. By the time the storm hit, every stake was double-deep, every pack was under a weighted tarp. The party slept through it.

onSuccess.narrative: You raised the alarm just in time. Tents staked, gear secured. The worst of the storm rages harmlessly overhead. Some grit in everything, but nothing lost.

onMixed.narrative: You caught the storm late. Most of the gear is secured, but the sand got into the food packs and one tent collapsed. A gritty, miserable night.

effects.description: Grit, noise, and collapsed shelter disrupted rest.

onFailure.narrative: The storm catches the camp unprepared. By morning, the camp is half-buried beneath dunes. Packs are full of grit. Some critical supplies have been scoured away or buried beyond recovery.

effects.description: Supplies buried or scoured away by the sandstorm.
Poor rest quality from sand-buried camp.

events.name: Scorpion Nest
events.gmPrompt: The sandy ground crunches strangely underfoot. As the firelight catches the shifting sand, pale shapes are moving. The camp was pitched directly over a nest of desert scorpions, and they are emerging to hunt in the cool night air.
events.checkContext: Perception check to spot the swarm and relocate the camp before anyone is stung.
events.gmGuidance: Not a combat encounter unless the GM escalates. These are mundane scorpions, not giant ones. The threat is a sting to a sleeping character, not a fight. Perception to spot them, then a quick relocation. auto_fail without a watch because nobody sees them until someone gets stung. Speak with Animals: scorpions are instinctual. No conversation. Hunt and flee only.
events.description: The camp was pitched over a scorpion nest. Pale shapes are emerging from the sand.

onTriumph.narrative: You noticed the first pale shape well before the main swarm surfaced. Everyone up, gear off the ground, camp relocated thirty yards upwind. Done before anyone got stung.

onSuccess.narrative: You spot the swelling swarm before anyone rolls onto them. The party relocates quickly. An unsettling hour, but no stings.

onMixed.narrative: You caught it, but not before one got into a boot. A painful sting, a panicked relocation, and a nervous night checking every fold of fabric.

effects.description: Nobody slept soundly after the scorpion scare.

onFailure.narrative: A sudden, sharp cry breaks the night. Stings and panic as the camp scrambles awake in the dark, shaking out blankets and boots. The venom burns.

events.name: Water Shortage
events.gmPrompt: The heat has not broken. Even hours after sunset the rocks are still radiating warmth like a furnace. Exposed skin is hot to the touch. Everyone is drinking more than planned, and the containers are emptying fast.
events.checkContext: Survival check to manage the extreme heat and limit water consumption.
events.gmGuidance: An unusually hot night, not a supply management failure. It works whether the party has two waterskins or twenty -- the heat forces extra consumption either way. Create Water or Goodberry cancels outright. A windbreak (defenses) cancels. The consume_resource effect goes through ResourceSink. If the party has no water, the noResourceFallback triggers exhaustion instead.
events.description: The heat has not broken. Everyone is drinking more than planned.

defenses.narrative: The windbreak reduced exposure. Water consumption stayed manageable.

onTriumph.narrative: You knew this heat was unusual. Damp cloths over mouths, shelter angled against the radiant rock, disciplined sips only. Everyone came through without dipping into reserves.

onSuccess.narrative: Through careful discipline and a damp cloth technique, the party manages the heat without burning through extra water.

onMixed.narrative: The heat wins the argument. Extra water consumed to keep everyone functional. Tomorrow's supply will be tighter.

noResourceFallback.description: No water available. Dehydration sets in.

effects.description: Extra water consumed to manage the heat.

onFailure.narrative: The heat is unrelenting. Extra water consumed and it still was not enough. Everyone wakes parched and sluggish. Facing the morning sun will be brutal.

effects.description: Extra water consumed, still not enough.
The heat broke them despite the water.

events.name: Buried Trade Goods
events.gmPrompt: While pacing the perimeter, the wind-scoured ground reveals the polished edge of something buried. It looks like old trade goods from a lost caravan, half swallowed by the encroaching dunes.
events.checkContext: Investigation check to excavate the cache without damaging the contents.
events.gmGuidance: Reward event. The cache is from a long-dead trade caravan. Contents are mundane but valuable: spices, preserved goods, maybe a sealed waterskin. Failure breaks the fragile containers, not a trap. Only fires if watch is set.
events.description: Wind-scoured ground reveals the edge of a buried cache. Old trade goods from a lost caravan.

onTriumph.narrative: Patient excavation. You clear the sand without disturbing the clay seals. Intact jars of preserved spices, a sealed waterskin still holding fluid, and a leather pouch of trade coins.

description.value: A handful of old coins recovered from a buried trade cache. Tarnished but spendable.

onSuccess.narrative: Carefully digging it out, you uncover several intact sealed clay jars holding preserved exotic spices.

description.value: A handful of old coins recovered from a buried trade cache. Tarnished but spendable.

onMixed.narrative: You pulled one jar free intact, but the rest crumbled. A single jar of spices, better than nothing.

description.value: A handful of old coins recovered from a buried trade cache. Tarnished but spendable.

onFailure.narrative: In your excitement, you pull too hard. The ancient clay jars crumble to dust in your hands, their contents blowing away in the night breeze. Nothing salvageable.

events.name: Desert Sky
events.gmPrompt: Without cloud or canopy, the vast night sky is overwhelming. Countless stars stretch from horizon to horizon. A shooting star traces a line across the void. The desert, for all its daytime cruelty, offers a profound serenity tonight. This is a quiet RP moment if the table wants it.
events.gmGuidance: Pure atmosphere. No mechanical consequence. If the table likes RP moments, this is a chance for characters to reflect, share a moment, or just take in the silence. If the table does not care for ambiance, skip it and move on. Either way, the rest is undisturbed.
events.description: The vast desert night sky is overwhelming. Countless stars stretch from horizon to horizon. A shooting star traces a line across the void.
